message:
feat(airtable): harden attachment downloads with SSRF protection and size limits

Rewrite download_attachment() to defend against server-side request
forgery (SSRF) and unbounded response consumption. The previous
implementation followed redirects implicitly and imposed no constraints
on the download target, allowing a malicious Airtable attachment URL to
redirect to internal network addresses or stream arbitrarily large
payloads.

Security controls:
- URL validation: reject non-HTTPS URLs (except localhost in test mode),
  block private/loopback IP addresses to prevent SSRF against internal
  services
- Redirect hardening: dedicated HTTP client with Policy::none() to
  manually follow redirects (max 5 hops), re-validating each redirect
  target against the allowlist before following
- Content-Length enforcement: reject responses declaring > 100MB before
  downloading any bytes (AttachmentTooLarge error)
- Query string scrubbing: error messages include the host but never
  leak signed URL query parameters (which contain auth tokens)
- Configurable timeouts: separate connect (30s) and total (120s)
  timeouts for attachment downloads vs API calls

New error variants:
- InvalidAttachmentUrl: URL validation failures (SSRF, non-HTTPS, etc.)
- AttachmentTooLarge: content-length exceeds 100MB limit
- Both map to FcpError::InvalidRequest with code 1003

Tests:
- Unit tests for Display and FcpError mapping of new error variants
- Integration tests: disallowed host rejection (no query leak),
  oversized content-length rejection, local redirect following,
  redirect chain to allowed hosts

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
